metadata.source: Godart & Olivier, GORILA Vol. 5 (1985), Études Crétoises 21
metadata.notes: Vol 5 contains three sign-related data sections: (1) Tableau des signes standardisés (standardized sign table), (2) Tableau des variantes des signes (sign variant atlas), and (3) Index des signes (sign index/concordance). The actual 'Planches des signes' fold-out plates were printed on microfiches (1)-(3), not in the bound volume. Sign variant data and sign index data extracted from available page images.

tableau_signes_standardises.title: Tableau des signes standardisés
tableau_signes_standardises.description: Master reference grid showing the standardized (canonical) form of each Linear A sign, organized by GORILA numbering system. Simple signs (AB series) come first, followed by complex/ligature signs (A series), then fractions.

organization.section_1: Signes simples (Simple signs) - AB 01 through AB 188+
organization.section_2: Signes complexes (Complex/ligature signs) - A 301 through A 700+

tableau_signes_standardises.grid_format: Each cell contains: sign drawing (canonical form), GORILA number below. Some cells note Linear B equivalents in parentheses.
tableau_signes_standardises.extraction_notes: Full sign grid requires vision extraction. Each page contains ~80-100 sign cells in a dense grid format.

tableau_variantes_des_signes.title: Tableau des variantes des signes
tableau_variantes_des_signes.description: Paleographic atlas showing all attested variant forms of each sign across all inscriptions. Each sign entry shows multiple hand-drawn variant forms with the inscription reference below each variant. This is the GORILA equivalent of what SigLA covers digitally.
tableau_variantes_des_signes.format: Each sign gets a bordered section with: sign number (AB XX or A XXX) centered at top, multiple hand-drawn variant forms arranged in rows, inscription reference below each variant (e.g., HT 5.3, KH 37.1). Signs in parentheses indicate uncertain readings.

sign_variants_extracted.note: Parentheses indicate uncertain reading for KN Zg 9.1
First A-series sign (transition from AB to A series)

tableau_variantes_des_signes.extraction_notes: 28 pages of sign variant data. Each page contains 3-8 sign entries with 2-15+ variants each. Full extraction requires systematic vision processing of all variant pages. Sample data above covers ~20 signs from pages 30, 35, 40, 45, 50.

index_des_signes.title: Index des signes
index_des_signes.description: Comprehensive index of all sign sequences (sign groups/words) in which each sign appears, with numeric transliteration codes and inscription references. This is the core analytical tool for studying sign distribution and co-occurrence patterns.

format.description: Each sign gets a section headed by its drawn form and GORILA number. Below are all sign sequences containing that sign, shown as: (1) drawn sign group on left, (2) numeric transliteration in center using GORILA numbers separated by hyphens, (3) inscription reference on right.
format.example_entry: For sign 03: drawn sequence of signs | 01-08-03 | ZA 16.4² 21a.6³

columns.description: Hand-drawn sign sequence containing the target sign
Function classification letter (F, G, H, etc.) when applicable
Numeric code sequence using GORILA numbers (e.g., 01-08-03)
Inscription IDs with volume superscripts (e.g., HT 38.2¹, ZA 4a.3⁴)

sample_entries_sign_67.note: Page 248 (printed page 67) shows extensive entries for this sign

index_des_signes.extraction_notes: 192 pages of dense sign index data. This is the single largest data section in all of GORILA. Each page contains entries for 1-3 signs with 10-50+ sign sequences each. Full extraction would yield thousands of sign sequence entries with inscription cross-references. This data is critical for computational analysis of Linear A sign distribution and would complement the lineara.xyz corpus data. Requires systematic OCR/vision processing.

planches_des_signes_microfiches.title: Planches des signes (Microfiches)
planches_des_signes_microfiches.description: The actual sign plates were published as 3 microfiches, NOT as pages in the bound volume. Pages 379-380 in the book contain only introductory text explaining the microfiche organization.
planches_des_signes_microfiches.organization_notes: Signs are recorded in the order and numbering from the Tableau des signes standardisés (pages XXII-XXVII). For documents at 1:1 scale, sign plates were produced from the facsimiles. For other documents, scale indication is noted. Signs are ordered: by sign number first, then alphabetically by site for tablets (Wa-Wc, Za-Zg). Attestations listed below each sign variant show inscription provenance.
planches_des_signes_microfiches.availability: Microfiches are separate physical media and are NOT included in the CEFAEL digital scans. Access to original microfiches would be required for full extraction.

sign_numbering_system.description: GORILA uses a dual numbering system for Linear A signs

AB_series.description: Signs shared with Linear B (AB = A and B). These are the syllabographic signs with known or suspected phonetic values from Linear B decipherment.

A_series.description: Signs unique to Linear A (not found in Linear B). These include logograms, complex signs, ligatures, and signs of unknown function.

B_series.description: Signs found only in Linear B but referenced in GORILA for comparison purposes.
